title: MilPay
summary: MCAAT Admin checklist - MilPay. 72 unique items across 3 audiences.
applicabilityLevel: IPAC, Reporting Unit, and Supporting Unit

source.title: MCAAT Admin Checklist - MilPay
source.publisher: Marine Corps Manpower and Reserve Affairs

items.question: Is Move in Housing Allowance (MIHA) (TTC 455) reported IAW applicable regulations and within 5 days after the effective date?
Is the Move-in Housing Allowance Claim (DD Form 2556) filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Is Cost of Living Allowance reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is Cost of Living Allowance form (NAVMC 11106) properly completed and filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Is Family Separation Allowance - Restricted (FSA-R) reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is the Statement to Substantiate Payment of Family Separation Allowance (FSA), DD Form 1561, properly completed and filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Are commuted rations reported IAW applicable regulations?
references: MCO 10110.47A, Chap. 1, para.  5.a-l (NAVMC 10522, Commuted Rations Action)

items.question: Is the Commuted Rations Action Form (NAVMC 10522) completed properly and filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
references: MCO 10110.47A, Chap. 1, par. 5. g-i, and l (NAVMC 10522, Commuted Rations Action)

items.question: Are updates to pay entry base dates PEBD (TTC 063-000) reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is Continuation Pay statement of understanding, NAVMC Form 11905 (Active) or 11908 (Reserve), filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Is the Blended Retirement System (BRS) Continuation Pay (CP) (TTC 450) reported IAW applicable regulations?
Are periods of leave and administrative absence (PTAD) properly reported via unit diary IAW applicable regulations?
Are approved BNA packages, and recertifications reported within 5 days of receiving key supporting documents?
Are contractual documents reported within 5 days of receiving key supporting documents?
Are contractual documents filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary? (i.e. DD Form 4, NAVMC 321A, and NAVMC 321M)?
Is approval obtained from MMIB prior to reporting BAH protection (TTC 393) or Continuation of Location (COL)?
Is MMIB approval for BAH protection or Continuation of Location (COL) filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Is BAH without dependents approved by the appropriate command authority? Note: Appropriate command authority will be defined by the local BAH without policy.
references: MCTFSPRIUM, Chap. 8, Sect. 2, Chap. 13, Appendix C

items.question: Is the approval for BAH without dependents filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Does the reporting unit obtain appropriate documentation prior to reporting BAH for a designated place other than the previous duty station for Marines assigned to an unaccompanied tour overseas?
Is the Basic Allowance for Housing/BAH-DIFF properly reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) properly reported IAW applicable regulations and within 5 days after the effective date?
Is the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) (DD Form 2367) filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Is Family Separation Housing Allowance (FSH) properly reported IAW applicable regulations?
Are the Family Separation Housing Allowance documents DD form 2367 filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the date reported on unit diary?
Is the Commuted Rations Action Form (NAVMC 10522) completed properly and filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the reporting date?
references: MCO 10110.47A, Chap. 1, par. 5. g-i, and l (NAVMC 10522, Commuted Rations Action)

items.question: Are commuted rations reported IAW applicable regulations?
references: MCO 10110.47A, Chap. 1, par. 5a and l (NAVMC 10522, Commuted Rations Action)

items.question: Is Cost of Living Allowance reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is Cost of Living Allowance form (NAVMC 11106) properly completed and filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Is Family Separation Allowance - Restricted (FSA-R) reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is the Statement To Substantiate Payment of Family Separation Allowance (FSA), DD Form 1561, properly completed and filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Is Move in Housing Allowance (MIHA) (TTC 455) reported IAW applicable regulations and within 5 days after the effective date?
Is the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) (DD Form 2367) filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Is the Move-in Housing Allowance Claim (DD Form 2556) filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Are the Family Separation Housing Allowance documents (DD form 2367) filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Are pay entitlements reported IAW applicable regulations when attached under a humanitarian status?
Is the command recertifying a Marine’s eligibility to BNA after the occurrence of a routine event outlined in the reference?
Is the command ensuring BNA notifications, Pg 11s and applications are submitted to the Marine’s OMPF?
Are periods of leave, special liberty, and administrative absence (PTAD) properly reported via the Marine online Leave/Liberty module or unit diary as appropriate?
Are contractual documents filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date? (i.e. DD Form 4, NAVMC 321A, and NAVMC 321M)?
Is the Blended Retirement System (BRS) Continuation Pay (CP) (TTC 450) reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is Continuation Pay statement of understanding, NAVMC Form 11905 (Active) or 11908 (Reserve), filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Are updates to pay entry base dates PEBD (TTC 063-000) reported IAW applicable regulations?
Are contractual documents reported within 5 days of the effective date?
Is approval obtained from MMIB prior to reporting BAH protection (TTC 393) or Continuation of Location (COL)?
Is MMIB approval for BAH protection or Continuation of Location (COL) filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Is BAH without dependents approved by the appropriate command authority? Note: Appropriate command authority will be defined by the installation BAH without policy.
references: MCTFSPRIUM, Chap. 8, Sect. 2, Chap. 13, Appendix C

items.question: Is the approval for BAH without dependents filed in OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Is the Basic Allowance for Housing/ BAH-DIFF properly reported IAW applicable regulations?
Is Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) reported IAW applicable regulations and within 5 days after the effective date?
Is Family Separation Housing Allowance (FSH) (DD form 2367) properly reported IAW applicable regulations and within 5 days after the effective date?
Is the unit ensuring its BAS policy is reviewed every 3 years?
Are approved BNA packages reported (TTC 694) within 5 days of receiving key supporting documents?
Are periods of leave, special liberty, and administrative absence (PTAD) authorized in accordance with regulations? This applies to all aspects of the Leave and Liberty Program.
Is the unit ensuring its BAS policy is reviewed every 3 years?

For Marines authorized commuted rations IAW the command's BAS policy, is the unit maintaining key supporting documents (i.e. Fleet Assistance Program orders, rifle or burial details, armory personnel, etc., or NAVMC 10522) on file for audit, and in accordance with retention policies?
Is the unit ensuring key supporting documents authorizing Marines commuted rations are submitted to and received by the reporting unit within 5 days of authorization?
Is the unit validating that Commuted Rations are reported IAW applicable regulations by the reporting unit?
Is the unit ensuring contractual documents NAVMC 321, Reenlistment DD Form 4, are submitted to and received by the reporting unit within 5 days of the sworn date or reenlistment date, respectively?
Is the unit validating contractual documents NAVMC 321, Reenlistment DD Form 4, are reported IAW regulations?
Is the unit ensuring key supporting documents that validate entitlement to basic pay (i.e., NAVMC 321 and Reenlistment DD Form 4) are in the OMPF within 10 working days from the effective date?
Are periods of leave, special liberty, and administrative absence (PTAD) properly reported via the Marine on Line Leave/Liberty module or unit diary as appropriate?
Is the command notifying pre-screened Marines, no later than 30 days after receipt of the MOL pre-screen advisory, utilizing the NAVMC 118-11, administrative remarks?
Is the command ensuring requests for BNA are submitted via MOL EPAR?
Are requests for BNA adjudicated by the O5 Commanding Officer or SCMCA? (This authority cannot be delegated below the O5 Commanding Officer/SMCA level).
After adjudication, is the command notifying Marines of the results and does the notification provides justification of the decision?
Are eligible and certified requests to receive BNA properly computed?
Are eligible and certified requests submitted to and received by the reporting unit no later than 5 days after certification?
Is the command verifying and submitting to the reporting unit BNA credits for each month a Marine is entitled?
Is the command ensuring BNA notifications, Pg 11s and applications are submitted to the Marine’s OMPF?
Is the command recertifying a Marine’s eligibility to BNA after the occurrence of a routine event outlined in the reference?
Is the command maintaining BNA records in accordance with regulations?
